content.text: Do seven (7) and only seven of the following.

body.text: Prove that a compact Hausdorff space is normal.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that \(A\) is dense in \(X\) if and only if every nonempty open subset \(V\) in \(X\) satisfies \(V\cap A\ne\emptyset\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Assume that \(X\) and \(Y\) are topological spaces with \(Y\) Hausdorff and \(A\) is dense in \(X\). Suppose that \(f:X\to Y\) and \(g:X\to Y\) are continuous functions with \(f(a)=g(a)\) for all \(a\in A\). Prove that \(f(x)=g(x)\) for all \(x\in X\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Is \(B\) open in the box topology?

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Is \(B\) open in the uniform topology?

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Is \(B\) open in the product topology?

body.text:
If \((X,d)\) is a metric space and \(A\subset X\), for \(x\in X\) define
\[
d(x,A)=\inf\{d(x,a):a\in A\}.
\]

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that the function \(f:X\to[0,\infty)\) defined by \(f(x)=d(x,A)\) is continuous.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that \(x\in\overline{A}\) if and only if \(f(x)=0\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: If \(A\) is compact then for any \(x\in X\) there exists an \(a\in A\) with \(d(x,A)=d(x,a)\).

body.text: Show that \((X,d)\) is complete if and only if any nested family of nonempty closed sets \(A_n\) with \(\operatorname{diam}(A_n)\to0\) has \(\bigcap A_n\ne\emptyset\).
Assume that \(f:X\to Y\) is continuous and surjective.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: If \(X\) is Lindelöf, show that \(Y\) is also.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: If \(X\) is separable, show that \(Y\) is also.

body.text: If \(X\) and \(Y\) are connected topological spaces, show that \(X\times Y\) is connected.
In the real line \(\mathbb{R}\) let \(\mathcal{T}\) be the collection of all subsets \(A\) of \(\mathbb{R}\) such that \(\mathbb{R}-A\) is either finite or all of \(\mathbb{R}\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: To which point or points does the sequence \(x_n=1/n^2\) converge?
